command: תקן את הצגת שמות אנשי הקשר ומספרי הטלפון ברשימת השיחות - השם צריך להיות מוצג בדיוק כמו שהוא מוצג ב-WhatsApp Web. אם איש הקשר שמור בטלפון, מציגים את השם השמור. אם לא שמור, מציגים את ה-push name. אם אין push name, מציגים את מספר הטלפון

plan.summary: Fix contact name and phone number display in conversations list to match WhatsApp Web's exact behavior (saved contact name → push name → phone number).

tasks.instruction: Update the backend API to fetch and return contact names exactly as WhatsApp Web does: prioritize saved contact name from WhatsApp, fallback to push name, then phone number. Ensure this logic is applied in the conversations list endpoint (e.g., /api/tenants/[tenantId]/conversations) and any related message/contact endpoints.
tasks.context: Current implementation may not handle WhatsApp contact name hierarchy correctly. Use Baileys session data in session-manager to get accurate contact info (saved name, push name). Update Supabase queries or in-memory logic to include this hierarchy. Reference: session-manager/src/session-manager.ts for contact data.

tasks.instruction: Update the frontend conversations list (ChatTab.tsx and related components) to display contact names based on the new backend data. Ensure it shows saved contact name, push name, or phone number in that order, matching WhatsApp Web. Handle RTL for Hebrew names and real-time updates via Supabase subscriptions.
tasks.context: Currently, conversations list might show inconsistent names. Use the updated backend API response. Ensure UI reflects changes immediately with Supabase real-time. Check components/tenant/ChatTab.tsx and any list rendering logic.

tasks.instruction: Review the contact name display in conversations list for clarity and alignment with WhatsApp Web's UX. Provide recommendations for fallback states (e.g., when only phone number is available) and ensure Hebrew RTL text is handled properly in the UI.
tasks.context: Focus on user experience: how names are truncated, formatted, and prioritized. Ensure consistency across the app. Reference WhatsApp Web as benchmark.

tasks.instruction: Review the updated backend logic for contact name fetching to ensure multi-tenant data isolation is maintained. Verify that contact data (saved names, push names) is only accessible to the correct tenant and that no cross-tenant leaks occur.
tasks.context: Contact names may be fetched from WhatsApp via Baileys; ensure session data is scoped to tenant. Check RLS policies in Supabase and API route auth checks.

tasks.instruction: Design test cases for the contact name display fix: verify saved contact name, push name, and phone number fallback hierarchy works correctly. Test with Hebrew names, real-time updates, and edge cases (no saved contact, no push name). Include multi-tenant isolation tests.
tasks.context: Test both backend API responses and frontend rendering. Use existing test data or simulate WhatsApp contact scenarios. Ensure no regressions in conversations list functionality.

synthesis.implementation_plan: 1. Create contact_details table with RLS policies (SQL migration). 2. Update session-manager/src/session-store.ts to store contact hierarchy (saved_name, push_name, verified_name) with tenant isolation. 3. Update session-manager/src/message-handler.ts to call updateContactName on incoming messages. 4. Update src/app/api/tenants/[tenantId]/conversations/route.ts to join contact_details and apply WhatsApp name hierarchy (saved_name → verified_name → push_name → contact_name → phone_number). 5. Update src/components/tenant/ChatTab.tsx to use new API response, add getDisplayName helper with RTL detection, and ensure real-time updates via Supabase subscription. 6. Apply same logic to other endpoints (messages, contacts). 7. Run backfill migration for existing conversations. 8. Test with Hebrew names, real-time updates, and multi-tenant isolation.
blockers: Contact details table migration must be deployed before code changes
Backend API changes must be deployed before frontend updates
Session manager must be updated to store contact hierarchy data
synthesis.pm_notes: Coordinate backend and frontend deployments to avoid breaking changes. Ensure RTL Hebrew support is tested thoroughly. Security review confirms tenant isolation is critical—verify RLS policies are correctly applied. QA should test cross-tenant access, real-time updates, and edge cases like long Hebrew names.

feedbacks.what_worked: Comprehensive analysis of current codebase, detailed implementation plan with proper WhatsApp hierarchy (saved name → verified name → push name → phone number), clear database schema with migrations, and integration with Baileys session manager. Good attention to real-time updates and fallback logic.
feedbacks.what_to_improve: Should verify the exact WhatsApp Web behavior more precisely (verified name may not be part of standard hierarchy). The API response structure could be simplified - returning raw contact details might expose unnecessary data. Missing error handling for edge cases like malformed JIDs.
feedbacks.memory_update: WhatsApp Web hierarchy is: saved contact name → push name → phone number (verified name is for business accounts only). Always validate JID format before extracting phone numbers.

feedbacks.what_worked: Excellent analysis of WhatsApp Web UX patterns, comprehensive RTL handling for Hebrew, practical CSS implementation, clear accessibility requirements, and thorough testing scenarios. Good attention to mobile-first design and visual hierarchy.
feedbacks.what_to_improve: Could provide more specific integration examples with the actual component structure. Some WhatsApp Web behaviors (like exact time formatting) might vary by region.
feedbacks.memory_update: Israeli phone number formatting: 050-xxx-xxxx for local display, but WhatsApp Web shows international format (+972...). Always test with actual WhatsApp Web side-by-side.

feedbacks.what_worked: Good practical implementation with helper functions, RTL detection, real-time subscription handling, and responsive CSS. Clear integration plan with existing ChatTab component.
feedbacks.what_to_improve: Phone formatting logic is incorrect for Israeli numbers (should be 050-xxx-xxxx, not +972 XX XXX XXXX). Missing proper TypeScript interfaces for conversation data. The RTL detection could be more robust (consider mixed Hebrew/English names). Should reference the actual backend response structure more accurately.
feedbacks.memory_update: Always use TypeScript interfaces for API responses. Phone number formatting should match local user expectations (Israeli format: 050-xxx-xxxx).

feedbacks.what_worked: Excellent identification of critical multi-tenant isolation risks, practical RLS policy implementation, clear compliance considerations (Israeli PDPA), and actionable security fixes. Good attention to session isolation and audit logging.
feedbacks.what_to_improve: Could mention specific Supabase RLS best practices for the stack. The contact_metadata table naming might conflict with existing schema.
feedbacks.memory_update: Always verify tenant ownership at both API route level AND database RLS level for defense in depth. Session data must be strictly tenant-isolated.

feedbacks.what_worked: Comprehensive test plan covering all scenarios (backend, frontend, real-time), excellent edge case identification, practical code examples with Jest and Playwright, and clear success criteria. Good attention to cross-tenant isolation testing.
feedbacks.what_to_improve: Test cases could include more WhatsApp-specific scenarios (like business verified names). Some test mocks could be more realistic for the actual stack.
feedbacks.memory_update: Always test the exact WhatsApp hierarchy: saved contact → push name → phone number. Include tests for concurrent real-time updates across multiple browser tabs.
